title: Real Madrid midfield target leaves door open to summer transfer: ‘As I said before…’
description: The FIFA World Cup final has two top midfield targets of Real Madrid going head-to-head against each other – Rodri and Enzo Fernandez.While the former is Spain’s captain and has been one of their ...
fullText:
The FIFA World Cup final has two top midfield targets of Real Madrid going head-to-head against each other – Rodri and Enzo Fernandez.

While the former is Spain’s captain and has been one of their key pieces in midfield, the latter has slowly improved as the tournament progressed and scored a crucial equaliser against England in the semifinal.

Real Madrid have publicly ruled out a move for Chelsea’s Fernandez, but Rodri reportedly remains an option for the club as reported by certain outlets.

The Spanish national team captain has been actively talking to the media ahead of his side’s World Cup final clash against Argentina tomorrow night, and questions about his club-level future also joined the mix.

When asked about his situation with Manchester City and whether he was considering leaving the club in this summer or the coming transfer windows, the Spanish midfielder did not rule out anything.

Heart of Spain’s midfield. (Photo by Kevin C. Cox/Getty Images)

“Right now, as I said before the World Cup, thinking about this, I’ve got a contract with my club and it’s something I’ll want to sort out when it’s all over,” he said on his future.

“The challenge we’re facing is important enough that we can’t afford to think about this just yet. Raising my game will help me make a decision,” he added.

“Obviously, I’m happy with my performance, but we’re just a step away from glory now,” he concluded, via Diario AS.

Needless to say, Rodri’s response was very neutral and lukewarm for a player under contract and it is clear that he is keeping all doors open regarding his future.

While he is not assured that he will stay in Manchester, he did not close doors to a shift in scenery.

Real Madrid, needless to say, will be alert to the development given that he is on their long list of targets.

Should he win the World Cup, there may be more concrete interest from the Merengues given Rodri’s quality and leadership.

Perez has his doubts on Rodri’s arrival. (Photo by Angel Martinez/Getty Images)

While Rodri has indeed been extensively linked with Real Madrid in recent days, especially after Spain’s qualification to the final, word has it that Los Blancos have not changed their stance on him.

As matters stand, Real Madrid have not made any approach towards Rodri or his entourage and remain against signing him this summer for a plethora of reasons.

Several prominent figures in the administration believe that Rodri is the right profile for Real Madrid and are placing internal pressure on Florentino Perez, but the president continues to hesitate against the move owing to his fitness struggles, age and price tag.

Needless to say, any progress will only take place after the World Cup final, and it remains to be seen what stance Los Blancos adopt towards signing Rodri.

title: Patriots Legend Tom Brady Slaps Logan Paul as WWE Rumors Swirl
description: Tom Brady spent two decades making defenses pay for giving him an opening.Logan Paul gave him one Friday at Fanatics Fest.The former New England Patriots quarterback appeared to slap the WWE star across the face during an onstage exchange at the Javits Center in New York.Video posted by WWE showed New York Knicks star Karl-Anthony […] The post Patriots Legend Tom Brady Slaps Logan Paul as WWE Rumors Swirl appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
Tom Brady spent two decades making defenses pay for giving him an opening.

Logan Paul gave him one Friday at Fanatics Fest.

The former New England Patriots quarterback appeared to slap the WWE star across the face during an onstage exchange at the Javits Center in New York.

Video posted by WWE showed New York Knicks star Karl-Anthony Towns standing between the two before Brady reached around him and connected with an open hand.

.@TomBrady just SLAPPED @LoganPaul at #FanaticsFest! 😱@Fanatics | @FanaticsFest pic.twitter.com/PdhHOInFPF

Towns quickly extended both arms and separated them.

Paul continued talking while Brady stared back from the other side.

The scene carried the look of a wrestling angle, from the convenient camera position to Paul’s exaggerated response. It also comes one day after Brady openly asked WWE to find him a match.

Whether the slap was scripted or improvised, Brady suddenly has the opponent and storyline he claimed to be waiting for.

Paul wrote afterward that the exchange began because he was teasing Brady over their March flag football meeting.

He accused Brady of setting a poor example and added that he had blocked him, all while pointing out that he is recovering from a torn triceps.

Brady answered with one word: “Dork.”

He followed with another post, writing, “I tried America … will try again next time I see this nerd,” per the San Francisco Chronicle.

The two have spent months building a public rivalry around Paul’s belief that his WWE background allows him to compete with elite football players.

Brady dismissed that argument before the Fanatics Flag Football Classic, and their teams later met during the March exhibition.

Paul’s Wildcats defeated Brady’s Founders team, though Team USA won the event.

Paul later released an “I’m Sorry” video aimed at Brady and others, continuing the celebration after the tournament, as Bleacher Report detailed.

Brady still praised Paul’s preparation and performance afterward, but that compliment didn’t slow the public back-and-forth.

Brady made his wrestling interest clear during a live appearance Thursday with Cody Rhodes on the “What Do You Wanna Talk About?” podcast.

The seven-time Super Bowl champion said he was “waiting for Nick Khan to come up with some storyline” and believed retirement had given him an opportunity to display his remaining athletic ability.

“Come on, Nick. Make it happen,” Brady said, via CBS Sports.

Paul and Brady would give WWE a celebrity showdown for the ages.

Paul has years of ring experience, understands how to guide a crossover attraction and already owns a ready-made history with Brady. His injury could delay any plans, with the Chronicle reporting that Paul expects to need another three to four months before returning.

Brady is also the kind of high-visibility character that the WWE loves to place at the center of a spectacle.

A six-time championship winner with the Patriots and another with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, Brady spent much of his career thriving as the villain in opposing stadiums.

The slap may have been another piece of Fanatics Fest theater.

But maybe it’s the first physical scene in a story that the WWE will continue whenever Paul is healthy enough to finish it.

Like HEAVY's content? Be sure to follow us.

This article was originally published on HEAVY

The post Patriots Legend Tom Brady Slaps Logan Paul as WWE Rumors Swirl appeared first on HEAVY.

title: LeBron James riles up 76ers fans with familiar phrase
description: At Fanatics Fest, LeBron James ruffled the feathers of 76ers fans by using a phrase they have often heard over the years.
fullText:
On Friday, LeBron James was at Fanatics Fest in New York City for the second day in a row. On Thursday, he did a live recording of his "Mind the Game" podcast at the event; this time, he did his show "The Shop" before a live audience.

As was the case on Thursday, James was urged to reveal which NBA team he will play for this coming season. Again, he said that there is no announcement to be made yet.

There were some Philadelphia 76ers fans in attendance when James was speaking, and, of course, the 76ers are thought to be one of the frontrunners for his services. James decided to ruffle their feathers when he explained one thing he's looking for in his next team, per Dave McMenamin of ESPN.

"Most important is I want to compete," James said. "I want to compete at a high level. I want to join a franchise that kind of shares the same motto as myself and that's practicing championship habits every day, but trusting the process more than anything. I think you trust the process of actually getting to that point …"

The venue erupted in both cheers and boos when James said, "Trust the process." That's the infamous phrase former Sixers executive Sam Hinkie used about a decade ago when the team started to rebuild, and it was later used by franchise player Joel Embiid.

Embiid was drafted in 2014, and over the years, "the process" has taken many twists and turns. This summer, Philly stunned the league by trading for Jaylen Brown, the five-time All-Star wing who helped lead the Boston Celtics to the NBA championship in 2024 and won finals MVP honors that year.

With Embiid, Brown and Tyrese Maxey, Philly may already have a championship-caliber team, even if Embiid, as he often does, misses dozens of regular-season games due to injury. As a result, many feel the 76ers would offer James his best shot at winning his fifth ring.

James claimed that he had used the phrase "trust the process" long before Hinkie started to.

"I've been saying 'trust the process' since I was drafted in, like, 2003," James said. "I don't know if Embiid was even born yet."

With that, the world continues to await where he will spend his 24th NBA season.

This article originally appeared on LeBron Wire: LeBron James riles up 76ers fans with familiar phrase

title: Benfica to rival Köln in race for West Ham's Edson Álvarez
description: SL Benfica have entered the race to sign West Ham United midfielder Edson Álvarez, according to Sky Germany.The presence of the Portuguese club ought to apply some uneasy pressure on 1.FC Köln who w...
fullText:
SL Benfica have entered the race to sign West Ham United midfielder Edson Álvarez, according to Sky Germany.

The presence of the Portuguese club ought to apply some uneasy pressure on 1.FC Köln who were hoping to sign the Mexican.

Regardless, FC Köln are still hoping to complete the deal, but the overall package remains too expensive at this stage.

It now remains to be seen what Benfica are prepared to put on the table and whether they can outmuscle the Bundesliga side.

Benfica may have turned their attention to the 28-year-old after they discovered that Bayern Munich's João Palhinha was out of their range.

Last season, Álvarez spent the year on loan from West Ham to Fenerbahçe where he made 12 appearances in the Süper Lig.

title: Talks held as Liverpool move closer to deal for £51m World Cup midfield powerhouse
description: Liverpool are reported to be shopping for midfielders this summer. We may need to see some outgoings before a new face is brought in. Fringe men like Stefan Bajcetic and Harvey Elliott have been linke...
fullText:
Liverpool are reported to be shopping for midfielders this summer.

We may need to see some outgoings before a new face is brought in. Fringe men like Stefan Bajcetic and Harvey Elliott have been linked with the exit door.

And Curtis Jones - wanted by Inter Milan - could depart once the Italian champions reach Richard Hughes’ €40m asking price.

Among the contenders named as Liverpool targets is Adam Wharton. The Crystal Palace midfielder, 22, has risen to prominence over the last couple of years and now looks set for a big move.

But the England international would cost £100m - meaning Liverpool could well be searching for other options.

Kaishu Sano, 25, is one such name.

The Mainz midfielder had a terrific FIFA World Cup for Japan with a report from TEAMtalk now stating that talks have been opened over a summer deal.

Liverpool would be prepared to pay up to £51m for the midfield powerhouse - hoping to beat off competition from Arsenal, Tottenham and others.

“The Reds are prepared to offer up to €60million (£51m / $69m) for the Mainz star after holding talks over a deal,” the report reads.

“They are not alone, and Arsenal, Tottenham and Borussia Dortmund are also tracking him with interest and have been in contact with his representatives.

“Liverpool are considering a bid that would easily represent a record sale for Mainz, if accepted.”

Kaishū Sano is a 25‑year‑old Japanese defensive midfielder, 1.76m, who joined Mainz from Kashima Antlers in July 2024 and has since become a guaranteed starter, barely missing a league minute across his first two Bundesliga seasons.

His Japan form at the 2026 World Cup, including a shock goal against Brazil, has raised his profile beyond Germany.

On the pitch, Sano is a classic midfield workhorse: relentless engine, huge coverage, tough tackling and intelligent positioning, breaking up play and recycling possession with simple, secure passing rather than headline‑grabbing creativity.

He embodies Mainz’s high‑intensity style, regularly leading the league for distance covered and intensive runs.

Contract‑wise, he’s tied to Mainz until 2028 after a €2.5m move, giving the club strong leverage as his value climbs.

Eintracht Frankfurt have already placed him on a shortlist, and his World Cup exposure makes a future step to a higher‑budget Bundesliga side or a mid‑table Premier League club – as a reliable ball‑winning No.6 – a realistic next move.

title: The six players Jose Mourinho considers the ‘core’ of his project – report
description: Arriving as Real Madrid’s new manager, Jose Mourinho was always going to shake up Real Madrid’s pecking order across all departments.The experienced manager, after all, has all the backing he can ...
fullText:
Arriving as Real Madrid’s new manager, Jose Mourinho was always going to shake up Real Madrid’s pecking order across all departments.

The experienced manager, after all, has all the backing he can ask for from the club president Florentino Perez and has even been handed a free hand in taking transfer decisions.

All that has been asked of him is to restore order in Real Madrid’s dressing room and bring the team back to winning titles, and Mourinho has been working on finding the best working combination on that front.

As reported by Diario AS in a recent report, six Real Madrid players are guaranteed to start under Jose Mourinho next season. The manager rates their profiles highly and sees them as a ‘core’ of his project.

The first name is Thibaut Courtois in goal, and unsurprisingly so given how the player has been ever so reliable despite being at the twilight of his career. He will continue to start in goal without any doubt.

Cucurella will be a starter in his first season at Real Madrid. (Photo by Shaun Botterill/Getty Images)

In defence, the only guaranteed starter is Marc Cucurella, the new left-back Real Madrid signed this summer. The player has had a stunning World Cup campaign and will replace Alvaro Carreras as the team’s first-choice left-back.

Two midfielders are guaranteed starters – Jude Bellingham and Federico Valverde. While the former is expected to play in an advanced attacking midfield role as he did in the World Cup, the Uruguayan will be deeper seated.

Finally, Kylian Mbappe and Vinicius Jr. are both assured a place in the team’s starting XI at centre-forward and left-wing respectively, and understandably so.

Unfortunately for Mourinho, a sizeable chunk of his ‘core’ players will be unavailable for most of pre-season, implying that he will have to directly count on the players for the beginning of the season without much preparation.

Valverde will be the first of ‘the six’ to return to Madrid. (Photo by David Ramos/Getty Images)

Valverde will be the first to return to pre-season from the aforementioned list given Uruguay’s group stage elimination, and he could well return by next week.

Vinicius Jr. and the Brazilian contingent will return to training at the end of the month, and Thibaut Courtois likely a week later given that Belgium progressed until the quarterfinal.

Mbappe, Bellingham and Cucurella, however, will only return just before the new season as they still have games to play in the competition.

While France and England will play for third place later today, Cucurella will play the final against Argentina tomorrow.

title: Youri Tielemans: Man United signing reveals reason behind celebration
description: Manchester United have completed three signings this week in what has been a very productive transfer window so far.Shock signingCompletely out of the blue, it was announced that Manchester United wer...
fullText:
Manchester United have completed three signings this week in what has been a very productive transfer window so far.

Completely out of the blue, it was announced that Manchester United were close to triggering Youri Tielemans‘ release clause at Aston Villa.

The Belgian subsequently completed the move this week with his delighted family beside him.

The former Leicester City man has already spoken about his desire to play for manager Michael Carrick.

He has also revealed that his former teammate and current first-team coach, Jonny Evans, was influential in his decision to move.

Tielemans may not score that often, but when he does, his goals are usually spectacular.

He did score two goals for Belgium in their exciting comeback win over Senegal in the World Cup.

Tielemans’ go-to celebration is holding his hands up in the shape of a heart, and speaking to the club’s official site, he explained why.

The 29-year-old explained, “that’s mainly for my family. They’re always there everywhere to support me, so I’m grateful to have them by my side. That’s a small gesture that I can give back to them.”

Tielemans has a wife and three young children, and claims that he tries to spend as much time as possible with them when he is not playing or training.

He asserted, “the main thing I do [away from football] is spending time with them. I just try to switch off when I’m at home and be the best dad I can be.”

The Belgian also explained that whilst he loves to score spectacular goals, he would be content to find the back of the net in any way for United.

Tielemans added, “a tap-in is as good as a worldie, but I’m just looking forward to showing my game, to show what I’m about and to have a good run with the team.”

Featured image Alex Grimm via Getty Images

The Peoples Person has been one of the world’s leading Man United news sites for over a decade. bsky.social

title: Lionel Messi reacts to iconic Lamine Yamal photo before World Cup final: ‘It’s crazy…’
description: Lionel Messi has described Lamine Yamal as one of the best players in world football ahead of Sunday’s World Cup final, but the Argentina captain admitted he hopes the Barcelona youngster is kept qu...
fullText:
Lionel Messi has described Lamine Yamal as one of the best players in world football ahead of Sunday’s World Cup final, but the Argentina captain admitted he hopes the Barcelona youngster is kept quiet when the two nations meet with football’s biggest prize at stake.

The meeting between Spain and Argentina has naturally been built around two generations of football’s elite.

On one side is Messi, preparing for another World Cup final with Argentina.

On the other is Yamal, whose performances have confirmed his status as one of the brightest talents in the game.

Away from the official press conferences, FIFA organised a special event at the Fan Fest featuring Spain coach Luis de la Fuente, midfielder Rodri, Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni and goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez.

The biggest attraction, however, was Messi, who also appeared alongside Novak Djokovic, Kevin Durant and Tom Brady for a discussion ahead of the final.

Messi has nothing but praise for Lamine Yamal. (Photo by Elsa/Getty Images)

Asked about the teenager’s rapid rise, Messi had nothing but admiration for the Barcelona winger, revealing he has followed his progress closely because of his affection for the Catalan club.

“Lamine is a truly great player, whom I’ve followed very closely because he plays for a club I love and for which I always wish the best.

“At just 19, he’s already one of the world’s leading players. He has his whole career ahead of him and the chance to achieve something historic.

“We’ll try to make sure it doesn’t happen this time,” said the Barcelona legend.

Messi was also asked about the famous photograph taken nearly two decades ago, showing him holding a baby Yamal during a charity calendar photoshoot.

The image has resurfaced repeatedly in recent months, especially with the pair now preparing to face each other on football’s biggest stage.

Reflecting on that moment, Messi said, “That photo is crazy. Life…

“I had my photo taken with him when I was a baby and now we’re set to face each other in a World Cup final.

“He’s one of the best in the world right now and I wish him the very best of luck because if he does well, so will Barcelona.

“That said, we’ll try to put in a good performance so he doesn’t play at his best, although that’s difficult.”

While his admiration for Yamal was clear, Messi also reminded everyone that Spain’s strength lies in the collective rather than one individual.

He concluded, “It’s not just Lamine. Spain have some brilliant players and play great football. We’ve got our own weapons too.”

title: A's Notes: Kotsay Reflects on Decision to Fire Pitching Coach Scott Emerson
description: Kotsay opens up on dismissing longtime pitching coach Scott Emerson, plus updates on Rooker's season-ending knee surgery, Severino's MRI, and more from the injury front
fullText:
The A's began their All-Star break by firing pitching coach Scott Emerson on Monday, after he'd been with the organization for over 20 years. Emerson, or "Emo" as he was called in the clubhouse, had been the A's pitching coach since 2017.

Friday on A's Cast, manager Mark Kotsay said that the decision to dismiss Emerson wasn't an easy one, but one that he and GM David Forst felt needed to be made.

"I'm still not through it, really," Kotsay said of the conversation he had with Emerson earlier this week, given how long the two have known each other and how long Emo had served as Kotsay's pitching coach in his managerial career thus far.

"I hold myself accountable," Kotsay continued. "Scott's a relentless worker. Scott cares about this organization as much as anything in his life. This discussion with him on Monday morning wasn't an easy one, because it wasn't for lack of effort. It wasn't for the work ethic that he puts into it day in and day out."

Replacing Emerson will be former bullpen coach Dan Hubbs, with Javy Guerra being promoted from Double-A Midland to the A's bullpen coach.

"Dan has relationships in that locker room. He has relationships with the whole bullpen," Kotsay said when asked about the expectations for Hubbs. "The opportunity is to go in, deep dive on some of the things that the players are looking for. The biggest thing for me is to simplify the information.

"We all know, and it's no secret, that this ballpark is challenging to pitch in. We've got to identify how to pitch in it better. The biggest thing for me is to pitch away from hard contact, pitch away from damage. The scouting reports have to be simple, but to the point as to where we can go when we can't give up damage."

Brent Rooker underwent successful left knee arthroscopic surgery today with Dr. Tim McAdams and Dr. Mike Freehill at Stanford Orthopedic Hospital in Redwood City, Calif. Dr. McAdams and Dr. Freehill debrided the articular cartilage tear and removed loose bodies from Rooker’s knee. He will be out for the remainder of the season and is expected to be ready for Spring Training 2027.

The full medical report for injured players will be released on Saturday, but right-hander Luis Severino, out since May 30 with a right shoulder strain, has undergone an MRI. The results of the MRI hadn't been read when Kotsay spoke to the media.

Kurtz has shown some progress, but with the players just getting back from the All-Star break, he hadn't been assessed yet.

Denzel Clarke is doing baseball activity — hitting, running, and throwing — along with some outfield work.

With the injuries to key players, Kotsay mentioned that this is now a time when young guys like Joshua Kuroda-Grauer, Henry Bolte, and Tommy White can integrate themselves into this group.

Martín Gallegos of MLB.com also provided an update on Ryan Lasko: "He was transferred yesterday to Kessler Institute for Rehabilitation in his home state of New Jersey to continue his rehab. There has been no change in his condition, though this is a step in the right direction and Lasko is in good spirits."

title: Atlanta Falcons Still Have Two Major Roster Questions Before 2026 Season
description: Despite an improved roster, the Atlanta Falcons still have key questions entering 2026. CBS Sports identified defensive tackle and wide receiver as the biggest remaining needs as Atlanta looks to end the NFL's second-longest active playoff drought.
fullText:
Does the Atlanta Falcons have a good enough roster to return to the NFL Playoffs in 2026?

The franchise did a great deal of work this offseason, acquiring talent and making some promising selections in the NFL Draft, but the team will still have to prove it on the field after missing the postseason every year since 2017.

However, if they want to break the second-longest playoff drought in the NFL, a few positions might need some upgrades. Recently, CBS Sports discussed what is still the biggest offseason priority for every team in the NFC, and unfortunately for the Falcons, they landed on more than one.

First, they believe the Falcons' defensive line is a major question mark and that they need to add more to the group before the season begins.

"The Falcons flipped defensive tackle Ruke Orhorhoro for Maason Smith. The team also has Brandon Dorlus and Da'Shawn Hand at the position. Edge rusher is a wild card with James Pearce Jr.'s situation."

Smith is the biggest question mark in determining whether this unit can dominate. The Falcons got Smith in a trade with the Jacksonville Jaguars this past April during the draft, and hopefully a change of scenery will be exactly what he needs. The former second-round pick has not been consistent yet, but the system under Jeff Ulbrich should improve that right away. If they are able to unlock more from Smith, there may not be an issue in terms of depth at the position after all.

Wide receiver was also mentioned as a need for offseason improvement, and after the contract extension to Drake London, that may seem puzzling to some. However, many are concerned about the lack of 'proof of concept' from the rest of the WR room.

"Wide receiver and defensive tackle are on similar footing. At receiver, the team added a few veterans, Jahan Dotson and Olamide Zaccheaus, but those are the only proven commodities aside from Drake London. Atlanta drafted Zachariah Branch in the third round, but he may not be ready to run the full route tree."

Having Branch ready to make an impact as early as week 1 against the Pittsburgh Steelers will certainly quiet a ton of critics regarding this position group. Still, the criticism also seems valid as the season approaches. Both Dotson and Zaccheaus have shown signs in the past at other stops that they can make an impact, but it's been very short-lived and not at the potential expense of being considered the second-best receiver on the squad.

If a player like Dotson can keep his normal production we have seen so far in his career, but with a much larger target share, something we are bound to see with this group in Atlanta, then the offense should be in really good shape.

title: Dana White not entertaining Conor McGregor vs. Max Holloway trilogy
description: Conor McGregor has no plans of walking away from fighting. The former two-division champion is planning another comeback after suffering a knee injury in the opening seconds of the UFC 329 main event on July 11. The Irishman faced former featherweight champion and former BMF titleholder Max ...
fullText:
Conor McGregor has no plans of walking away from fighting. The former two-division champion is planning another comeback after suffering a knee injury in the opening seconds of the UFC 329 main event on July 11.

The Irishman faced former featherweight champion and former BMF titleholder Max Holloway in a rematch. At the sound of the bell to start the bout, McGregor rushed across the cage and leaped into the air with a jumping switch kick. He landed awkwardly on his right leg and fell to the canvas. He fell twice more, once while throwing a kick and later a punch. The referee stepped in. The fight lasted 69 seconds.

McGregor stated that he'll require surgery but will return. The 38 year-old wants to settle things with Holloway when he does but UFC CEO Dana White isn't entertaining any matchup for McGregor at the moment.

"Conor still has to have knee surgery and go through everything that he's going to have to go through," White said during an appearance on the Spinnin Backfist MMA Show. "And I understand. Listen, he's, you know, he's very unhappy with the way sh*t played out, but you're a human being. You're older. You haven't fought in five years, and your body. Listen, Father Time is undefeated. Okay?"

Even if McGregor is able to return, the Irishman will be sidelined for an extended period of time. The landscape of the sport could be drastically different when and if "The Notorious" is able to stage a comeback.

"Who the f*ck knows what's going to happen Max over the next, you know, year and what's going to happen to Conor over the next year. So, I don't even think about that type of stuff until it comes a reality," White said.

"When Conor reaches out and says, "Uh, I finished all of my physical therapy, leg's is good place. They told me I can start training full-time now." Then you start thinking about what's next. ... I'm not even thinking about any of that stuff because it's so long and so far away it doesn't matter."

title: Most World Cups hosted by country: Mexico, Brazil lead full list of FIFA locations since 1930
description: Here is a look at the countries that have hosted the World Cup most often, along with a year-by-year list of every host since the tournament began in 1930.
fullText:
Most World Cups hosted by country: Mexico, Brazil lead full list of FIFA locations since 1930 originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The World Cup took place in Canada, Mexico and the United States in 2026, the first time three nations have shared hosting duties for the tournament.

For Mexico, it's been a landmark moment. By staging matches in 2026, it became the first country to host the men's World Cup three times, having previously welcomed the world in 1970 and 1986. Its Estadio Azteca also became the first stadium to feature at three separate World Cups.

Here is a look at the countries that have hosted the World Cup most often, along with a year-by-year list of every host since the tournament began in 1930.

2026 WORLD CUP HQ:Latest World Cup news | Full World Cup schedule

Just one country has hosted the men's World Cup three times, while a cluster of soccer's traditional powers have staged it twice. Mexico's hat-trick of tournaments puts it clear at the top of the list.

Below are the nations that have hosted, or are set to host, the World Cup on multiple occasions.

Germany's first turn came in 1974, when it hosted as West Germany. Spain will become a two-time host in 2030, when it shares the centenary edition with Portugal and Morocco. The United States, meanwhile, joins the two-time club courtesy of its role in the 2026 tournament, having hosted alone in 1994.

A total of 18 different countries have hosted, or been awarded, the World Cup across its history, with several co-hosting arrangements in the modern era. They are as follows, by year:

Canada, Mexico and United States

*2030 also includes three matches across Argentina, Paraguay and Uruguay as part of the competition's centenary celebrations.

Co-hosting is a relatively recent development. South Korea and Japan became the first nations to share a World Cup in 2002, before the 2026 edition expanded the idea to three countries. The 2030 tournament will go further still, with Morocco, Portugal and Spain as the main hosts and one match each played in Uruguay, Argentina and Paraguay to mark 100 years since the inaugural World Cup.

Expert World Cup Round of 32 predictions

Ranking remaining World Cup teams 1-32

3 sleepers to watch in World Cup knockouts

Best teams out of the World Cup knockouts

The tournament has been held every four years without fail since 1950, but two editions were lost to the Second World War.

The 1942 World Cup was never staged, and the 1946 edition was also abandoned as the world recovered from the conflict.

That left a 12-year gap between the 1938 tournament in France and the 1950 World Cup in Brazil, the longest interruption in the competition's history.

Saudi Arabia will close the current cycle as the 2034 host, with FIFA yet to confirm any locations beyond that.

title: 'Played cricket equivalent to T20': Former England stars recall fond memories of Brendon McCullum's revolutionary Bazball
description: Following his sudden sacking by the ECB, former England stars recall fond memories of Brendon McCullum's revolutionary, T20-style "Bazball" Test era.
fullText:
'Played cricket equivalent to T20': Former England stars recall fond memories of Brendon McCullum's revolutionary Bazball originally appeared on Cricket News. Add Cricket News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Former England cricketers praise Brendon McCullum for reviving Test cricket's excitement.

McCullum leaves after mixed record, sacked following New Zealand series loss.

Off-field controversies and dwindling results marked his final months.

A host of former England cricketers, including Michael Vaughan, Alastair Cook, David Lloyd and Phil Tufnell, have looked back with genuine fondness at the era Brendon McCullum built through his now-famous Bazball approach, crediting the New Zealander for injecting fresh life into Test cricket during his time in charge.

McCullum took over England's Test setup back in 2022, and paired with skipper Ben Stokes, oversaw a stunning stretch of 10 victories from his opening 11 matches, with the team embracing a brand of aggressive, fearless cricket rarely seen before at that level.

However, that early magic gradually faded, and he departed the role without having beaten either Australia or India in a series. By the numbers, his overall record as Test coach reads 27 wins, two draws and 20 losses, with seven of those defeats arriving in just the last nine matches.

MORE: 'Real mess': Alastair Cook opens up on England cricket chaos amid Brendon McCullum leaving Test coaching

The England and Wales Cricket Board relieved McCullum of his Test duties following the recent home series loss to New Zealand.

He continues, however, to hold his position as head coach of England's white-ball setup, with his contract running until the conclusion of the 50-over World Cup in southern Africa in autumn 2027.

The unravelling truly began with a heavy 4-1 Ashes loss in Australia, after which McCullum, Stokes and cricket director Rob Key were initially retained in their roles. It was ultimately a 2-1 home series defeat to New Zealand that proved decisive, first costing Stokes his place and then McCullum his job, while Key remains in position with continued backing from ECB chief executive Richard Gould.

MORE: RCB's Andy Flower explains why he has ruled himself out of England coaching role

Speaking about his time in charge, Alastair Cook remembered McCullum simply for constantly pushing boundaries, while Phil Tufnell agreed enthusiastically, recalling how Bazball brought back genuine joy to Test cricket and left him stunned by what he was witnessing in its early days.

"Resurrecting the joy of test match cricket. I still remember the start of Bazball and everything. I was like, wow, what is going on here?" Tufnell commented.

David Lloyd summed up the era by noting that cricket became must-watch entertainment precisely because it resembled T20 cricket.

"Cricket was big news because it was sexy. And they played cricket equivalent to T20. They've run out of steam. They had to change. And they didn't change," he said.

MORE: Inside England's reported nine-man shortlist for Test coach role: The favourite, dark horse and outsiders

"Resurrecting the joy of Test match cricket." ❤️ 🏏

What was Brendon McCullum's best moment as England Test coach? 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 pic.twitter.com/FgGDVSpQm9

— Stick to Cricket (@StickToCricket) July 17, 2026

McCullum deserves genuine credit here. Test cricket was becoming a hard sell before Bazball arrived, and he made it must-watch television again, even if the philosophy eventually collided with reality against tougher opposition.

Entertainment and results aren't always the same thing, and his inability to beat India or Australia in a series remains a real blemish. Still, judging him purely on the scoreboard misses the point.

He changed how an entire generation thinks about batting under pressure, and that cultural shift will outlast his tenure.

The ECB now faces a tricky balancing act: finding a successor who can retain that fearless spirit while fixing the tactical gaps that turned strength into weakness once the Bazball era ran its course.

For all the latest cricket news, opinion, and commentary and to share your voice, head to our Facebook, Instagram, and X (Twitter) pages.

title: David Ornstein: Newcastle United have £46m bid rejected for top transfer target
description: Newcastle United See Lucas Bergvall Bid Rejected by TottenhamNewcastle United have made their move for Lucas Bergvall, and Tottenham have shut the door, for now. According to David Ornstein for The At...
fullText:
Newcastle United have made their move for Lucas Bergvall, and Tottenham have shut the door, for now. According to David Ornstein for The Athletic, Spurs have rejected an offer worth around £46million for the 20-year-old midfielder, with the report stating the bid “exceeded the approaches made by Nottingham Forest to date”.

That matters, because it tells you two things straight away. First, Newcastle are serious. Second, Tottenham still think there is more value in the player, whether that means keeping him or extracting a bigger fee. The key line is simple, “all attempts to sign the 20-year-old so far, including higher proposals, have been rejected by Spurs”, though “a sale would be considered if the figures reach a level they deem suitable.” That is not a no, it is a price-setting exercise.

Bergvall’s own position sharpens the story. He “has informed the club of his preference to seek an opportunity elsewhere this summer”, despite being under contract until June 2031. Long contract, limited leverage, familiar modern situation. If the player wants out, the selling club can still dictate terms, especially when there is more than one interested buyer.

Newcastle’s need is obvious. They sold Sandro Tonali to Spurs earlier in the window and also “missed out on the signing of Switzerland international Johan Manzambi, who moved to Aston Villa instead.” Bergvall fits the profile, young, technical, upward curve, resale value. He has made 78 appearances for Spurs since arriving in a £8.5m deal from Djurgarden in 2024, and last season he played 33 times, with one goal and five assists.

Tottenham, meanwhile, have already strengthened in midfield through Mateus Fernandes and Tonali. That usually creates room for movement, but only at the seller’s number, not the buyer’s. Newcastle have been told exactly where they stand.

From a Newcastle perspective, this one is intriguing because it looks like the club are trying to be aggressive without being reckless. Bergvall is young, clearly talented, and the fact he wants a new opportunity this summer changes the dynamic. If that is genuine, then Newcastle should keep pushing, but only to a point.

£46million is already serious money for a player with one goal and five assists last season, especially one coming off an ankle issue in January. Talent is there, no doubt, but there is a difference between paying for upside and overpaying because the selling club senses desperation. Tottenham have spent heavily, so naturally they are in no rush. They can posture. Newcastle do not need to play along forever.

The encouraging part is that the recruitment team appear to have a clear midfield plan after losing Tonali and missing out on Manzambi. Bergvall feels like a player who could grow quickly in the right setup, and at 20 there is obvious long-term value. Supporters will like that logic.

Still, if Spurs want silly money, walk away. Simple as that. Newcastle have to be smarter than that in this market. Bergvall is interesting, maybe very interesting, but he is not the only midfielder in Europe. If the fee climbs too far beyond £46million, the sensible move is to test Tottenham one more time, then move on fast.

title: The Open third round tee times including Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau
description: DeChambeau will play over the weekend ‘fired up’ by a controversial two-stroke penalty
fullText:
It’s moving day at Royal Birkdale as the contenders assemble for a fascinating third round at The Open.

Friday saw plenty of low scoring and a dollop of controversy, with Australia’s Lucas Herbert and the USA’s Sam Burns, a growing major force, shooting 62s to surge into contention.

Bryson DeChambeau looked perfectly poised, too, only to be hit with a two-stroke penalty after an incident on the fifth – the two-time major winner has confirmed he will play this weekend, though admitted he had been “fired up” by the decision.

He’ll be off alongside Burns in the third from last group, with Rory McIlroy, Scottie Scheffler, Tommy Fleetwood and Jon Rahm among those right in the mix but needing a strong Saturday to challenge.

title: Exclusive: ‘He is ready’ – Spain legend in no doubt that Andoni Iraola can ‘deliver’ for Liverpool
description: Former Spain midfielder Gaizka Mendieta is convinced that Andoni Iraola is ‘ready for the challenge’ which awaits him as Liverpool’s new head coach.The 44-year-old has earned a crack at the Anfi...
fullText:
Former Spain midfielder Gaizka Mendieta is convinced that Andoni Iraola is ‘ready for the challenge’ which awaits him as Liverpool’s new head coach.

The 44-year-old has earned a crack at the Anfield job off the back of his excellent work at Bournemouth, who achieved a first-ever qualification for Europe under his watch last season, finishing just one position and three points shy of the Reds.

DOWNLOAD THE OFFICIAL EMPIRE OF THE KOP APP FOR ALL THE LATEST & BREAKING UPDATES – STRAIGHT TO YOUR PHONE! ON APPLE & GOOGLE PLAY

Some pundits have questioned whether the Basque has what it takes to succeed at a club of LFC’s stature, but his appointment was said to have been very well received by several senior players in L4.

Mendieta firmly believes that Iraola can go on to be successful at Liverpool, backing the new boss to ‘handle the pressure’ of the job and put his three years of Premier League experience to good use at Anfield.

The former Valencia midfielder told Empire of the Kop (via football betting site BetVictor): “I know Andoni Iraola well, and he can handle the pressure. He is ready for the challenge at Liverpool. While you cannot promise trophies, he can quickly bring a clear style of football and identity to the club.

“His experience in the Premier League has taught him what is required. His approach to the market and his tactical ideas will make Liverpool competitive against Arsenal, Manchester City and Chelsea. He is ready and will deliver.”

Want more Empire of the Kop coverage? Add us as a preferred source on Google to your favourites list for news you can trust

Iraola will ultimately be judged by results on the pitch, but he couldn’t have made a better first impression in his introductory press conference last Monday, with Lewis Steele rightly describing the 44-year-old’s replies as ‘note perfect‘.

It was noteworthy that, right from the get-go, he hasn’t been afraid to publicly implore FSG to back him in the transfer market, insisting that he needs 

He won widespread plaudits for the intense, high-pressing style of play that he implemented at Bournemouth, and that proactive tactical approach is sure to be very well received by Kopites if it’s transferred across to Anfield.

His Cherries side were unbeaten across their final 18 Premier League matches last season, beating Arsenal and Liverpool in that run and also claiming draws against both Manchester clubs, and that achievement doesn’t seem to have been given the recognition it deserves.

Expectations will be far higher on Merseyside, of course, and he’ll have to get used to an unprecedented level of external scrutiny in his new role, but Iraola’s first press conference suggested that he’s well able to handle that side of the job.

Let’s hope that Mendieta’s strong faith in the 44-year-old to succeed at Anfield is well-placed and that anyone doubting the appointment will quickly be silenced.

title: NFL players want more grass fields after World Cup
description: NFL players start a social media campaign to support a call by their players union for more permanent grass fields following the World Cup.
fullText:
Some NFL players have started a social media campaign to support a call by their players union for more permanent grass fields following the World Cup.

Seven NFL stadiums in the US had grass fields installed for the duration of the tournament to meet the demands of organisers Fifa.

They are set to revert to artificial turf after the World Cup, despite 92% of NFL players preferring natural grass, according to the NFL Players Association (NFLPA).

Data shows that "artificial turf is significantly harder on the body than grass".

Fifteen of the NFL's 30 regular stadiums use grass, with the other half using artificial turf. One of the reasons for artificial turf is the increased cost to maintain grass.

Dozens of NFL players have posted messages with #WorthTheCost, including Chicago Bears quarterback Caleb Williams, who said: "Why can't we have this too?"

Indianapolis Colts running back Jonathan Taylor said: "Natural grass isn't out of reach. Prioritizing players shouldn't be either."

Green Bay Packers linebacker Zaire Franklin said: "The cost of doing nothing is paid for by players' bodies. Make grass mandatory."

An NFLPA statement issued just before the World Cup read: "It's no longer a question of capability; the technology exists, the expertise exists and the resources exist to install the high-level grass fields that our players overwhelmingly prefer.

"We have seen the meaningful investments made to meet the standard for international athletes and global events.

"NFL players - who regularly compete on these fields, help fund these stadiums and whose work makes the league what it is today - deserve the same commitment to quality grass fields."

title: Report: Liverpool star set for huge international opportunities
description: Rio Ngumoha England Chance Gives Liverpool Fresh Hope Under Andoni IraolaLiverpool supporters have seen enough false dawns in young players to know better than to get carried away. Even so, there is s...
fullText:
Liverpool supporters have seen enough false dawns in young players to know better than to get carried away. Even so, there is something about Rio Ngumoha that demands attention. According to The Telegraph, the teenager is firmly in Thomas Tuchel’s thinking as England shape their plans for Euro 2028. That matters, because elite international managers do not hand out encouragement lightly.

Ngumoha’s rise has been rapid since arriving from Chelsea in September 2024. Pre-season hints soon became first-team evidence. His Premier League debut against Newcastle United in August 2025 brought a dramatic finish, a goal in the 100th minute, a 3-2 win, and a place in Liverpool history as the club’s youngest scorer at 16 years and 361 days. He followed that by becoming Liverpool’s youngest player in European competition, then signed his first professional contract, then started a league game against Tottenham at Anfield. The sequence tells its own story.

The report says Ngumoha’s time with England in Florida left a strong impression on Tuchel and his staff. He travelled initially as a training player and still forced his way into a senior debut against New Zealand. That is a serious step for a player of 17. England camps are full of good youngsters. Very few make themselves impossible to ignore.

It also says plenty that he was the only training player to win a cap during those friendlies, despite the company around him. That underlines his standing and suggests this is more than a passing look from Tuchel. England are building towards Euro 2028, and Ngumoha has clearly put himself on the map.

For Liverpool, the issue is simple. Talent needs minutes. Ngumoha made five Premier League starts last season, but his pathway narrowed badly under Arne Slot as the campaign drifted. Interest from Bayern Munich and others was no surprise. Top clubs circle when young talent is underused.

Now the picture changes under Andoni Iraola. His reputation for trusting and improving young players is one reason this report feels significant. If Ngumoha gets proper first-team football at Anfield, the England conversation becomes far more real. If he does not, Liverpool risk wasting momentum that has been hard earned.

From a Liverpool perspective, this is exactly the sort of report you want to read in July. Not hype for the sake of it, not empty academy chatter, but recognition from one of the biggest stages in the game. If England’s set-up think Rio Ngumoha belongs in the discussion already, then Liverpool have a gem on their hands.

What should excite supporters most is the timing. Iraola arrives with a reputation for courage, energy and giving young players a proper chance. Ngumoha does not need wrapping in cotton wool forever. He needs coaching, sensible handling and, above all else, opportunities. The lad has already shown he can affect big moments. That late winner against Newcastle was not just a nice headline, it was proof of nerve.

There is also a wider point here for Liverpool. Elite clubs need their academy pathway to feel real. When a youngster looks ready, the manager has to be brave enough to trust him. If Iraola does that with Ngumoha, Liverpool could have a thrilling left-sided option and England could have another major attacking talent on the rise. Supporters should be pleased, and a little excited too. This feels like the start of something, provided the club handle it properly.

title: Real Madrid’s stance on Premier League star unchanged despite World Cup heroics
description: Enzo Fernandez was one of Argentina’s heroes in their comeback win over England in the semifinal of the FIFA World Cup earlier this week, and it is not surprising that the Spanish media have taken m...
fullText:
Enzo Fernandez was one of Argentina’s heroes in their comeback win over England in the semifinal of the FIFA World Cup earlier this week, and it is not surprising that the Spanish media have taken much interest in the development.

After all, the player was extensively linked with a move to Real Madrid this summer and even labelled as the club’s ‘top priority’ midfielder target.

Jose Mourinho has been pushing for the board to make one big midfield signing ahead of the new season, especially given how dull the department looked for most of last season with a sheer lack of creativity. Fernandez, on that front, was a profile he admired.

Los Blancos, however, went on to release an official statement that they are not interested in the player and that they did not make any formal offer, closing the door on his potential signing.

As reported by MARCA, Real Madrid’s stance on Fernandez has not changed despite the player’s stunning performances at the World Cup nor by his participation in the final of the event for the second consecutive edition.

Fernandez scored the crucial equaliser against England. (Photo by Todd Kirkland/Getty Images)

Enzo does want to leave Chelsea this summer, but Real Madrid have not made any approach for the star Argentine midfielder.

The player continues to dream of playing for Real Madrid and would be open to joining Los Blancos but there is a low to no possibility of that materialising at this point in time unless there is a radical change in situation.

At this point in time, there remains a massive doubt if Real Madrid will even pursue a new arrival in the middle of the park, and even the media consistently present conflicting reports.

Word has it that Jose Mourinho is still demanding a new face in midfield who can offer a creative outlet for the team.

Will Mourinho push for a new midfield signing? (Picture credit: Real Madrid official website)

However, the operation is completely dependent on departures, which are not close to materialising at the moment.

Should Real Madrid manage to sell Eduardo Camavinga, a case to push for a new arrival would arise and take precedence.

If the player manages to convince Mourinho in pre-season, however, there is a good chance the same core will stay for next season.

At this point, Real Madrid will instead look to prioritise any funds they have into the potential signing of Michael Olise, which is expected to exceed the €200 million mark.

Pulling off a second transfer like Fernandez’s, exceeding €100 million in the same window, needless to say, is unfeasible.

title: Aldana Abruptly Retires
description: Former UFC title challenger Irene Aldana is no longer on the UFC roster. On Friday, MMA Fighting reported that the 38-year-old Aldana was no longer eligible for a UFC ranking, removing her from her previous position in the Top 15. Given her ranking and layoff, the news of her release was met with surprise and […]
fullText:
Former UFC title challenger Irene Aldana is no longer on the UFC roster.

On Friday, MMA Fighting reported that the 38-year-old Aldana was no longer eligible for a UFC ranking, removing her from her previous position in the Top 15. Given her ranking and layoff, the news of her release was met with surprise and confusion. Shortly afterward, ESPN Deportes confirmed that Aldana has retired from competition in order to focus on her health.

In her final UFC appearance at Noche UFC 2024, Aldana suffered a unanimous decision loss to Norma Dumont that cost her position in the women’s Bantamweight Top Five. In the process, she suffered one of the nastiest cuts in UFC history (see it here). Since then, the injury has required multiple surgeries and remains a factor in her decision to retire.

Aldana (15-8 overall, 8-5 UFC) accomplished quite a bit in her decade on the roster. Her pinnacle was unquestionable fighting for UFC gold opposite Amanda Nunes, even if she came up short … like just about everyone else who faced a prime “Lioness.” Outside of challenging for the belt, Aldana picked up some incredible knockouts, like her ridiculous left hook KO of Ketlen Vieira or that wonky upkick to the body stoppage of Macy Chiasson.

title: 'Do we need Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma?': R Ashwin breaks silence on India's 2027 World Cup plans
description: Ravichandran Ashwin questions the timing of speculation about Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's ODI futures. He believes selectors should have communicated any transition plans much earlier. Ashwin stated that the experienced duo's presence is still needed for upcoming tours. Honest communication is essential when managing senior players' careers. Such discussions are surfacing late, with the next World Cup approaching.
fullText:
Amid speculation over Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's ODI futures, former India off-spinner Ravichandran Ashwin has questioned the timing of the debate, insisting that if the selectors wanted to move on from the veteran duo ahead of the 2027 ODI World Cup, they should have communicated that decision much earlier.

In recent weeks, social media has been abuzz with speculation that either Rohit Sharma or Virat Kohli could be dropped from India's ODI plans before the 2027 World Cup. Media reports even claimed that the third ODI at Lord's could be Rohit's final appearance in the format.

However, BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia dismissed those rumours while speaking to PTI on Friday, confirming that Rohit will continue to represent India in ODIs and rubbishing the speculation surrounding his retirement.

"There are lot of speculations going on in media about Rohit Sharma's future. I want to firmly assert that there has been no such discussion that Rohit will be playing his last match at Lord's on Sunday," said Saikia, as quoted by PTI.

Speaking on his YouTube channel Ash Ki Baat, Ashwin said India still needs the experience of Rohit and Virat for the upcoming tour of South Africa, but stressed that if the team management had decided to move on from them, the players should have been informed immediately after the Champions Trophy rather than allowing uncertainty to linger.

"I am thinking we are going to South Africa in September and October. Do we need Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma? If you ask me, I will say we need that experience. I know Yashasvi Jaiswal is pushing hard from behind, but if you ever felt as a selector, coach or captain that we don't want these players in the 2027 World Cup, then you could have communicated that at the end of the Champions Trophy," Ashwin Said.

Ashwin, who himself retired from international cricket after being informed that the team management wanted to look beyond him, said honest communication is essential when dealing with senior players.

"If you go to a player and tell him that we want to move on from you, no player will like that. Even when the coach came and communicated with me in Australia that they wanted to look past me, I accepted it. One bad series can happen to any player, but you have to communicate with the player."

The 39-year-old also questioned why such discussions are surfacing now, with less than a year remaining before the 2027 ODI World Cup.

"Beyond all the speculation, Rohit and Virat have been performing. If you really wanted to make such a transition, why has this discussion come only now, in August 2026, when there are only 10 to 12 months left before the Cricket World Cup?"

title: What is BTS? Meet the members, songs and more about 2026 World Cup halftime performers
description: Here's the rundown on the seven artists that make up BTS.
fullText:
What is BTS? Meet the members, songs and more about 2026 World Cup halftime performers originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

BTS made headlines recently as the K-pop band became one of four major participants in the first-ever World Cup halftime show.

Halftime shows, although extremely popular at major American sports events, are not as common around the world. FIFA swung for the fences in landing talent, and the seven-man group that is BTS was a great addition in order to garner global attention.

While most fans can pick a familiar name or two from the list, not everyone is knowledgeable about BTS before they perform on Sunday at MetLife Stadium.

Here's the rundown on the seven artists that make up BTS.

2026 WORLD CUP HQ:Latest World Cup news | Full World Cup schedule | Updated World Cup bracket

BTS, which is an acronym of Bangtan Sonyeondan or “Beyond the Scene,” is a Grammy-nominated South Korean boy band that first debuted in June 2013. Seven separate artists make up the collective group that has achieved worldwide stardom.

In 2020, BTS was nominated for the Grammy Award in Best Pop Duo/Group Performance. This earned them the title of the first South Korean pop artist to be nominated for the award, according to Yonhap News Agency.

According to Kworb, BTS has garnered over 53.1 billion streams on Spotify alone. Their top song, "Dynamite," has totaled 2.3 billion streams since its release in 2020. The group's most recent album, "ARIRANG", came out on March 20, 2026.

Odds, spread for Spain vs. Argentina World Cup final

World Cup prize money breakdown

BTS currently has seven members in their K-pop group. Here is a little bit about each member who makes up the group:

Jin, whose full name is Kim Seok-jin, was born on December 4, 1992, in Anyang, South Korea. The 33-year-old primarily serves as one of the four vocalists in the group.

He is also nicknamed "Worldwide Handsome," according to his Spotify page, and many fans are drawn to his smooth voice.

His most notable solo song, "Don't Say You Love Me," has garnered over 970 million streams on Spotify.

Suga, also known as Min Yoon-gi, was born on March 9, 1993, in Daegu, South Korea. The 33-year-old primarily serves as one of the three rappers in BTS.

The rapper has appeared on numerous other songs as a feature from notable artists around the world, including Juice WRLD, Halsey, and PSY. He has also helped produce the official ringtone for Samsung Electronics' Galaxy, "Over the Horizon."

According to Kworb, Suga has accumulated over 1.5 billion solo Spotify streams across his discography.

J-Hope, whose real name is Jung Ho-seok, was born on February 18, 1994, in Gwangju, South Korea. The 32-year-old is also one of the three rappers in BTS.

According to his personal Spotify page, J-Hope is the lead dancer for the group. In 2022, j-hope made history as the first South Korean act to headline a main stage at Lollapalooza, a major annual music festival in Chicago.

J-Hope currently has 4.4 billion solo Spotify streams to his name, according to Kworb.

RM, whose real name is Kim Nam-joon, was born on September 12, 1994, in Seoul, South Korea. The 31-year-old is also one of the three rappers in BTS and is regarded as the leader of the group.

RM, which has been said to stand for both "Real Me" and "Rap Monster," was the winner of the Favorite K-Pop Artist accolade at the 2025 American Music Awards.

His most popular song, "Wild Flower," has been listened to over 435 million times and has made up a sizeable chunk of RM's 3.3 billion solo streams on Spotify, per Kworb.

Jimin, also known as Park Ji-min, was born on October 13, 1995, in Busan, South Korea. The 30-year-old primarily serves as one of the four vocalists in BTS.

The Busan native serves as a hybrid in terms of his talents as a singer and dancer. In March 2023, his first solo album, FACE, made him the first South Korean solo artist to top the Billboard Hot 100, per his Spotify page.

Jimin has 8.4 billion solo streams on Spotify, according to Kworb. A total of 2.4 billion of those streams came from his song, "Who."

V, whose real name is Kim Tae-hyung, was born on December 30, 1995, in Daegu, South Korea. The 30-year-old is one of the four vocalists in BTS.

Along with making music for BTS, V has written some of his own songs in his discography. He has also participated in original soundtracks for various Korean TV series, such as “4 O’CLOCK,” “Scenery,” “Winter Bear,” “Blue & Grey,” and “Snow Flower,” according to his Spotify page.

His most popular song, "Love Me Again," has been listened to over 1.1 billion times, and has made up a sizeable chunk of V's 5.8 billion solo streams on Spotify, per Kworb.

Jung Kook, whose full name is Jeon Jung-kook, was born on September 1, 1997, in Busan, South Korea. The 28-year-old primarily serves as one of the four vocalists in the group.

The singer comfortably puts up the top numbers across the BTS group on a solo medium. Per his personal Spotify page, Jung Kook is known as an "all-rounder" due to his versatility in vocals, rap, and dance.

He has four songs that have garnered over a billion streams on Spotify, according to Kworb, including three billion from "Seven." In total, Jung Kook has accumulated 11.2 billion solo Spotify streams.

Prior to the World Cup halftime show, BTS has either been a primary or featured artist on 319 total songs, according to Kworb. The K-pop group has found a plethora of success since it first began in 2013, including over 53 billion total Spotify streams.

Here are the top songs from BTS in terms of streams on Spotify, per Kworb:

* Left and Right (Feat. Jung Kook of BTS)
